Empires & Puzzles Candy Hero Review: The Mirthful Medic

Candy brings the circus to the battlefield with a unique healing mechanic that mimics one of the game's strongest 5-star healers. But in a world of boosted health and overhealing, does this cheerful clown still have the last laugh in 3-star tournaments?
Candy: The Mirthful Medic - High-Stakes Healing in a Tiny Package
Candy, The Mirthful Medic: High-Stakes Healing in a Tiny Package
Candy is a Rare (3-star) Holy healer from the

event. Released back in 2021, she introduced a mechanic previously reserved for the legendary hero Raffaele to the 3-star arena: the "triage" heal. Her primary role is to salvage a team on the brink of death and then sustain them with regeneration. While the 3-star Holy roster has expanded significantly with powerhouses like Rekhetre and Dawn (who isn't a healer but dominates the slot), Candy remains a distinct option for players looking for substantial recovery potential. She is essentially a "Mini-Raffaele," bringing high-level mechanics to the rare tier.

Candy’s design is a vibrant explosion of circus tropes. She embodies the "harlequin" or court jester archetype with a classic motley outfit in red, green, and gold. Her expression is manic and joyful, fitting the somewhat chaotic energy of the Starfall Circus family. Unlike the grim warriors or terrifying monsters often found in Empires & Puzzles, Candy looks like she's genuinely having a great time, possibly unaware she's in a life-or-death battle. The art perfectly captures her "Whimsical Aid" skill name—she's here to help, but in her own theatrical way.

When Candy was released, her Health stat (809) was considered very high for a 3-star hero. In the current meta, she sits in the "Average to Good" tier. Modern 3-star heroes, especially those from the Construct family or recent Costumes, often push stats significantly higher (some breaking 900+ HP easily with emblems and Limit Breaks). Her Defense (488) is somewhat soft by modern standards, meaning she needs protection to ensure she survives long enough to fire her Average speed skill.
Whimsical Aid is a two-part healing skill that operates differently from standard "Heal 30%" abilities.
It sets the health of any ally below 50% HP

to 50%. This is massive for critical saves. If a hero is at 1 HP, this instantly restores them to half health. However, if a hero is at 51% HP, this part of the skill does absolutely nothing for them.
All allies regenerate 261 HP over 3 turns (87 HP per turn). This is a strong Heal Over Time (HoT) for the 3-star level, providing sustained recovery.
This combination makes her incredible at saving a losing match but less effective at "topping off" a healthy team compared to boosted health healers like Rekhetre.
Average. She requires 10 tiles to charge (or 9 with a high-level mana troop/bonuses, though 3-star mana troops are limited).
Scenario A (Ally at 10% HP, Max HP 1000):
Instantly heals to 500 HP (+400 immediate gain). Then adds 261 HP over 3 turns.
Scenario B (Ally at 80% HP, Max HP 1000):
Instant heal does nothing. Adds 261 HP over 3 turns.
A standard 35% healer would heal 350 HP in both scenarios. Candy wins dramatically when your team is dying but loses when you are winning.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Starfall Circus heroes rely heavily on their family bonus to maintain momentum.

The Starfall Circus family bonus provides a chance to cleanse the hero's most recent status ailment at the end of the turn. For 3-star tournaments, where cleansers are rare (and often slow), having a self-cleansing healer is a subtle but powerful advantage. It can shake off defense downs or burns without needing to fire a special skill.

is a solid Aether Power for Candy. Since she is a healer, her primary job is to survive until she can cast her spell. A 20% defense buff at the start of the battle helps mitigate her somewhat average defense stat against opening boards.

Upon release, Candy was a top-tier pick for Rare events and tournaments. The yellow 3-star pool lacked a dedicated, high-output healer (mostly relying on limited options or off-color healers). She filled a crucial gap.

(even though he's Green) shifted the meta. Rekhetre offers Boosted Health (overhealing), which is generally preferred because it’s never "wasted." Candy's heal is capped at 50% for the direct portion, meaning she cannot overheal.

, particularly in Buff Booster tournaments (due to the HoT buff) and as a depth option in the Contest of Elements (Holy). However, she is no longer the undisputed queen of yellow rare healers. She serves as excellent backup or a strategic pick when you expect to take heavy damage.

The family bonus makes her slippery against DOT (Damage over Time) teams.

The HoT buff counts as a buff for Buff Booster tournaments.
Unlike modern healers, she cannot boost HP above the max, leaving teams vulnerable to one-shots even after she fires.

She reacts to damage rather than preventing it.
Her regeneration counts as a buff, increasing attack for the team while keeping them alive.
Essential depth for the later stages where you need multiple healers.
The Triage heal counters the "permanent damage" or curses effectively by resetting the baseline, though boosted health is usually safer.
Great for auto-farming where the AI often lets health drop low before firing skills.
A double healer setup (one boosted, one triage) ensures that if the team gets low, Candy saves them, and if they are high, Rekhetre overheals them. Kvasir provides minion meat shields.
Candy keeps the attackers alive and provides an attack-boosting buff (via tournament rules). Whacker exploits the buffs for massive damage.

Candy is a fantastic pull for early-game players. A 3-star healer is essential for completing Rare Challenge Events and quests. Level her immediately if you lack a solid yellow healer. She will be your backbone for Rare quests for a long time.

If you have a fully emblemed and Limit Broken Rekhetre, Candy might see less use. However, do not feed her away. She is valuable for the Contest of Elements and as a backup in War cleanups using 3-star teams. She doesn't necessarily need the second Limit Break (Alpha Aether) unless you use her competitively in top 1% tournament defenses.
When Candy was released, the community immediately drew comparisons to the 5-star hero Raffaele.
"She is a Raffaele in minature. Brings a hero back from the dead."
There was significant discussion on whether her "50% cap" was a liability. Players realized she shines when things are going
, whereas other healers shine when maintaining a lead.
Many players noted she is excellent for the "Contest of Elements" simply because Holy lacks accessible healers.

Candy is the clown who stops the tears. While she may not offer the flashy "boosted health" of the latest power-creep heroes, her ability to instantly reset a battle that is going south is a unique and powerful tool. She forces the opponent to kill your heroes in one shot, because if they leave them at 1 HP, Candy will just undo all that hard work. She remains a solid, reliable, and sometimes frustratingly effective healer in the Rare arena.
As of this review, Candy does not have a costume, but her base form remains distinct enough to warrant a roster spot.
Keep one copy. Level her for Tournaments and Elemental Quests. She's a lifesaver—literally.
A deep dive into Candy, the Rare Holy Healer from the Starfall Circus family. Is she a mini-Raffaele for 3-star tournaments? We analyze her stats, special skill, and viability in the current meta.
